w:r.w:t: Master Product Requirements Document • Enterprise Design System • Implementation Spec
Classification: WORLD-CLASS TARGET • Version 4.0 • April 2025 • All Instructions Binding
Stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite 5 + Framer Motion + Tailwind CSS + Zustand
Theme: Glassmorphism (Light) / Deep Obsidian (Dark) / Bloomberg Terminal (Pro Mode)
Motorway Oil Enterprise v4.0 — UI/UX Architecture Overhaul Plan
React 18 · TypeScript · Vite 5 · Framer Motion · Tailwind CSS · Zustand
Bloomberg Terminal Aesthetic + Glassmorphism + Deep Obsidian Dark Mode
Three-mode: Glassy Light / Deep Obsidian Dark / Bloomberg Pro Terminal
Global theming · Layout components · Core component library · Animation system
Sidebar · GlobalHeader · MobileBottomNav · Layout · ReportViewer · All data grids
Motorway Oil Enterprise v4.0 UI/UX Overhaul Master Plan
Design Token System — Colors, Typography, Spacing, Motion

Motorway Oil Enterprise v4.0 represents a fundamental transformation of the application's visual identity and interactive experience. This is not an incremental UI refresh — it is a complete architectural overhaul that elevates the product from a functional business tool to a world-class, visually stunning enterprise platform that can compete and win at the global product excellence level.

The overhaul is inspired by the most respected visual languages in professional software: Bloomberg Terminal's information density and precision, Apple's glassmorphism and micro-interaction philosophy, and Linear's clean, ultra-fast dark-mode aesthetic. The result will be a product that looks and feels like a $500,000 enterprise system — built for a fraction of the cost and deployable to any petroleum retailer in the world.
Bloomberg Terminal Aesthetic = Maximum information density + zero visual clutter + precision color coding.
Every color has semantic meaning. Blue = primary data. Green = positive/up. Red = negative/alert. Amber = warning/pending.
Typography is tight, precise, and monospaced where data is shown. Proportional for headings and navigation.
The overall impression must be: "This is a serious tool built by serious engineers for serious business."
Every pixel must earn its place. Bloomberg Terminal users trust the interface because it shows maximum data without clutter. For Motorway Oil Enterprise, this means:
Tight line heights in data tables (44px row height maximum)
Condensed but readable typography at all data zoom levels
KPI cards that show value + trend + delta in one compact unit
Sidebar that collapses to icon-only without losing functionality
Dashboard grids using 12-column layouts, never wasted whitespace
Color is not decoration — it is data. Every color choice must have semantic meaning:
Blue (#2563EB) — Primary data, active states, selected items
Emerald (#059669) — Positive values, profits, successful operations
Rose (#E11D48) — Negative values, losses, alerts, critical states
Amber (#D97706) — Warnings, pending items, medium-priority alerts
Violet (#7C3AED) — Premium features, reports, enterprise data
The interface must feel alive. Users should receive constant, subtle feedback that their actions are registered and the system is responsive:
Hover states on every interactive element (< 150ms response)
Loading states with skeleton shimmer, not blank screens
Success states with brief green pulse animation
Error states with red shake animation (accessibility-safe)
Number counters that animate from 0 to value on mount
Chart data that draws progressively, not instantly
DARK MODE (default): Deep Obsidian (#060B18) base + translucent glass cards + vivid accent colors + subtle glow shadows.
LIGHT MODE: Crisp white (#F8FAFC) base + frosted glass surfaces + muted accents + clean drop shadows.
BLOOMBERG PRO MODE: Pure black (#000000) base + amber/cyan data colors + monospace font for all data.
CSS variables, color tokens, spacing scale, z-index system
Tailwind custom colors, shadows, animations, keyframes
Base HTML elements: body, scrollbars, selection color, focus rings
Glass, gradient, glow, shimmer, mesh utility classes
Card, Button, Input, Badge, Chip base styles
Information density, semantic color coding, data precision
Outdated typography, keypad-focused UX, no touch support
Ultra-fast dark mode, tight spacing, keyboard navigation feel
Glassmorphism, frosted panels, smooth spring animations
Clean deployment UI, status indicators, real-time streaming feel
Over-reliance on white space that wastes screen real estate
Panel-based layout, resizable sidebars, professional tool feel
Complexity appropriate for design tools but not data entry
Financial data presentation, subtle color coding, clean tables
SaaS-focused patterns that don't fit petroleum operations
Design Token System — Colors, Typography, Spacing, Motion

Every color used in the application must come from this token table. Hardcoded hex values in component files are forbidden. All colors are accessed via CSS variables (--color-name) or Tailwind custom color names.
Hero numbers on dashboards, KPI cards
All animations must use these standardized duration and easing tokens. Inconsistent timings break the sense of a cohesive, premium product.
Hover color changes, icon color shifts
Chart data draw animations, counter animations
Framer Motion sidebar, mobile nav pop

The existing :root / .dark two-class system is extended to a three-class system: :root (light), .dark (deep obsidian), and .bloomberg (terminal pro). All three share the same variable names — only the values change. This means zero component-level theme logic is needed.
Three theme classes: :root (Glassy White) · .dark (Deep Obsidian) · .bloomberg (Terminal Pro)
Applied to <html> element by ThemeContext based on user preference stored in useSettingsStore
System preference (@media prefers-color-scheme: dark) respected as initial default
Theme transition: all CSS variables transition over 300ms via body { transition: background-color 0.3s, color 0.3s }
No inline styles allowed in components — all theming must use CSS variables or Tailwind theme tokens
background: var(--bg-elevated); backdrop-filter: blur(20px); border: 1px solid var(--border-accent); border-radius: 16px;
Primary card surface for all panels
background: rgba(255,255,255,0.04); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border: 1px solid var(--border-accent); backdrop-filter: blur(8px);
All form inputs in dark mode
border: 1px solid transparent; background-clip: padding-box; position: relative;
width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--accent-emerald); animation: pulse-dot 2s infinite;
tailwind.config.js must be extended with the following custom tokens to make all custom utilities available as Tailwind classes:
colors: { obsidian: { DEFAULT: "#060B18", 50: "#1A2D5A" }, bloomberg: { bg: "#000000", card: "#0D0D0D", accent: "#F5A623" } }
animation: { "shimmer": "shimmer 1.5s infinite", "pulse-dot": "pulse-dot 2s ease-in-out infinite", "float": "float 3s ease-in-out infinite", "draw": "draw 0.6s ease-out forwards" }
The Sidebar is the primary navigation spine of the application. It must transform from a static list into a premium, animated, glassy panel that defines the visual identity of the entire platform.
Convert to fixed/absolute positioning with width transition: 280px (expanded) ↔ 72px (collapsed)
Apply glass-card class as base: backdrop-blur(24px) + border-right: 1px solid var(--border-accent)
Add mesh gradient overlay inside sidebar for depth: radial-gradient subtle blue tint at top
Wrap entire sidebar in Framer Motion <motion.aside> with LayoutGroup for smooth width animation
Logo area: full logo text when expanded, icon-only monogram when collapsed (animated crossfade)
Business unit switcher: pill-style tabs (Fuel / CNG / Lube) with animated active indicator that slides
Navigation items: Framer Motion hover state with translateX(4px) + glow-blue box-shadow on active
Active item: left-border accent (4px solid var(--accent-blue)) + glass-panel background fill
Icons: Lucide React with dual-tone — default color: var(--text-secondary), active: var(--accent-blue)
Item labels: AnimatePresence with slide-right + fade when collapsing (opacity 0 + translateX(-8px))
Collapse toggle button: animated chevron icon (180° rotation) at bottom of sidebar
Submenus: Framer Motion height animation (0 → auto) with staggerChildren for sub-items
User profile card at bottom: avatar + name + role badge when expanded, avatar-only when collapsed
Mobile: sidebar becomes a drawer with backdrop overlay (bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm)
Sidebar width, label visibility, logo form
Active item highlight, sliding indicator position
Which parent nav items have open submenus
Active business unit pill and filtered nav items
Background: backdrop-blur(24px) + background: var(--bg-elevated) + border-right: 1px solid var(--border-accent)
Top section height: 72px (matches header) with logo centered vertically
Nav item height: 44px with 8px vertical padding + 16px horizontal padding
Active item left border: 3px solid var(--accent-blue) (applied via ::before pseudo-element)
Active item background: rgba(37,99,235,0.12) in dark mode / rgba(37,99,235,0.08) in light mode
Icon size: 20px × 20px for all nav icons
Section separator: 1px solid var(--border) with 8px margin top and bottom
Hover background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05) in dark / rgba(0,0,0,0.04) in light
The GlobalHeader is permanently visible at the top of every page. It must convey platform professionalism, provide essential navigation context, and deliver real-time business status at a glance.
Sticky positioning: position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: var(--z-header)
Glassy background: backdrop-blur(24px) + background: var(--bg-elevated) + border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-accent)
Scroll-aware shadow: box-shadow appears only after scrollY > 10px (via scroll event listener)
Left section: breadcrumb trail showing Module > Page with animated fade when route changes
Center section: live business metrics ticker (today's sales / current shift status) in Bloomberg style
Right section: notification bell + theme toggle + user profile dropdown
Notification bell: badge count with scale-bounce animation when new notification arrives
Theme toggle: animated sun/moon icon with 360° rotation transition on switch
User avatar: ring border that pulses cyan when user is in an active shift
User dropdown: glass-card panel with slide-down + fade animation on open
Height: exactly 64px (--header-height token) to align perfectly with sidebar logo area
Mobile: hamburger menu replaces breadcrumb; compact layout with essential controls only
In Bloomberg Pro mode, the header center section displays a horizontal scrolling ticker of live business metrics — identical in feel to a Bloomberg Terminal data strip.
Emerald if positive, Rose if negative
The mobile bottom navigation bar must match iOS/Android premium app standards. Frosted glass, smooth animations, clear active states — it must feel native even though it is a web app.
Base style: position: fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; height: 72px (--mobile-nav-height)
Background: backdrop-filter: blur(24px) + saturate(180%) — matching iOS control center frosted glass
Safe area: padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for iPhone home indicator clearance
Border: border-top: 1px solid var(--border-accent) with subtle glow-blue on active item
Layout: 5 equally-spaced items (Home, Sales, Reports, Inventory, Settings)
Each item: Framer Motion whileTap={{ scale: 0.85 }} "pop" press animation
Active state: glowing pill background (rounded-full) behind icon, icon color = accent-blue
Active pill: Framer Motion layoutId="mobileActivePill" — slides smoothly between items
Icon size: 24px × 24px, label font-size: 10px below icon
Inactive icon: color = text-secondary, no background
Business unit indicator: small colored dot above active module icon (blue=Fuel, green=CNG, amber=Lube)

Layout.tsx wraps every page. Adding Framer Motion AnimatePresence here means every single route navigation gets a smooth, professional page transition — without any individual page needing to know about animations.
Wrap the main <Outlet /> in <AnimatePresence mode="wait"> to handle enter/exit transitions
Each page receives a <motion.div> wrapper with: initial, animate, exit variants
Transition variant: FADE_UP — { initial: {opacity:0, y:16}, animate: {opacity:1, y:0}, exit: {opacity:0, y:-8} }
Transition duration: 250ms enter, 150ms exit (exit is always faster)
Add subtle mesh-gradient background to the main container (not individual pages)
Mesh gradient: radial-gradient(ellipse 80% 60% at 50% -10%, rgba(37,99,235,0.08), transparent) layered on --bg-primary
Add a thin (2px) progress bar at top of page during navigation — electric blue color
The progress bar uses framer-motion scaleX from 0 → 1 over route transition duration
Sidebar and header do NOT participate in page transitions — they remain static for stability
Each module (Fuel/CNG/Lube) has its own subtle accent color in the mesh gradient
Page transition variant name: FADE_UP (default for all routes)
Optional variants: SLIDE_LEFT (for forward navigation), SLIDE_RIGHT (for back navigation), FADE (for modals)
Key prop on motion.div must be location.pathname to trigger exit/enter on route change
Reduced motion: respects prefers-reduced-motion — all transitions disabled, instant swap instead
Background mesh changes color subtly based on active businessUnit: blue (Fuel), green (CNG), amber (Lube)
The report viewer is the most frequently used component in the platform. It must combine the information density of Bloomberg with the visual polish of a premium SaaS dashboard.
Table header: background = var(--bg-surface) with sticky positioning (sticky top-0 z-10)
Header row: font-weight: 600, font-size: 13px, color: var(--text-secondary), letter-spacing: 0.04em
Data rows: height 44px, font-size: 14px, font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums
Row hover: transition background 80ms to rgba(37,99,235,0.06) in dark / rgba(0,0,0,0.03) in light
Alternating rows: very subtle stripe — even rows white/midnight, odd rows slightly elevated
Positive values: class data-positive (emerald color, tabular nums)
Negative values: class data-negative (rose color, tabular nums)
Sort indicator: animated arrow icon that rotates 180° on direction change
Column resize: drag handle on right edge of each header cell with cursor-col-resize
Export button cluster: Excel (green) / PDF (red) / Print (slate) — glass-card style buttons
Totals row: pinned to bottom with top border 2px solid var(--border-accent) and bold styling
Empty state: centered illustration with message and date range suggestion
Loading state: skeleton shimmer rows (8 rows of animated shimmer bars)
Search input: glass-input style with magnifier icon and instant client-side filter
Pagination: compact pill-style page selector with prev/next arrows and page count
The Digital Seal shown on printed/validated reports must be visually upgraded to premium status — it must look like a real official seal, not a plain badge.
VALID state: pulsing emerald glow (glow-emerald class) + animated ring that expands and fades
VALID text: emerald color + lock icon (Lucide Lock) + timestamp in monospace font
INVALID state: rose glow + static (no pulse — invalid states should not pulse)
Seal border: gradient border — linear-gradient(135°, emerald, cyan) for VALID
Background: glass-card with heavier blur (32px) for premium frosted look
Print mode: all glow effects removed, solid border used instead (print-color-adjust: exact)
Import pattern: import { motion, AnimatePresence, LayoutGroup } from "framer-motion"
Global variants file: src/animations/variants.ts — exports all reusable animation variants
Reduced motion hook: const { prefersReducedMotion } = useReducedMotion() — disables all animations if true
Performance: use transform and opacity ONLY for animations (GPU-composited, no layout reflow)
Never animate: width, height, top, left, margin, padding directly — use scale/translate instead
User avatar ring in active shift
color flash to accent then back
Three-Mode Theme System: Light / Dark / Bloomberg Pro

QuantumOps Enterprise / Motorway Oil Enterprise v4.0 supports three distinct visual themes. Each is a complete, fully-specified visual identity — not just a color swap. All three must render perfectly on every page, every component, and every report.
Clean whites + frosted glass + vivid accent colors + soft drop shadows
Power users, night use, focus work
Deep #060B18 base + translucent glass cards + vivid neon accents + glow shadows
Finance teams, data analysts, board reporting
Pure black base + amber/cyan data colors + monospace font + maximum density
Applied class: .bloomberg on <html> element (via ThemeContext toggle)
Background: #000000 (pure black) — not dark gray, not navy — pure black only
ALL numerical data: font-family JetBrains Mono (monospace) — this is the Bloomberg signature
Positive values: #00FF41 (terminal green) — bright, saturated, unmistakable
Primary accent: #F5A623 (amber/gold) — Bloomberg terminal signature amber
Live data indicators: #00D4FF (neon cyan) — used sparingly for real-time values only
Headings: #E5E5E5 (off-white, not pure white — reduces eye strain on pure black)
NO gradients in Bloomberg mode — flat colors only for maximum data clarity
NO blur/glass effects in Bloomberg mode — solid surfaces only for crispness
NO rounded corners larger than 4px — sharp, precise, terminal aesthetic
NO animations except counter animations — Bloomberg users want data, not motion
GlobalHeader right section + Settings page + keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+T
On first load, check prefers-color-scheme — map dark→"dark", light→"light"
ThemeContext applies class to document.documentElement (html element)
body { transition: background-color 0.3s ease, color 0.3s ease } — smooth swap
Small "PRO" amber badge in header when Bloomberg mode is active
Sun icon (light) / Moon icon (dark) / Terminal icon ⌨ (bloomberg)
Theme icon rotates 360° on switch (250ms spring)
Glassmorphism is a precise technique — not just "add blur everywhere." The following rules define the correct and incorrect use of glass effects throughout the application.
backdrop-filter: blur(20px) on floating surfaces (cards, sidebar, modals, header)
blur() on every element — excessive blur destroys contrast and readability
rgba(10,22,40,0.75) dark / rgba(255,255,255,0.75) light — 75% opacity minimum
100% opacity (no glass effect) or <50% (too transparent, unreadable)
1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08) in dark / rgba(0,0,0,0.06) in light
Hard solid borders that break the glass illusion
2–3 glass layers maximum: app bg → card → elevated card
More than 3 glass layers — visual noise, performance cost
Always have a rich gradient or mesh behind glass elements (glass is transparent)
Glass on plain flat color — no transparency effect visible
Ensure minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio — add text-shadow if needed
Light text on light glass or dark text on dark glass — unreadable
Use will-change: transform on glass elements that animate
backdrop-filter on hundreds of small elements — destroys GPU performance
The following new components must be created as part of the v4.0 overhaul. Each is a reusable primitive that will be used across Fuel, CNG, and Lube modules.
Dashboard stat card with animated counter, trend arrow, delta badge, and optional sparkline
animated number counter from 0→value on mount; trend arrow rotates based on positive/negative delta; sparkline uses SVG draw animation; glow border on cards with critical KPIs
Base glass morphism card container used by all panels
backdrop-blur(20px) background; border: 1px solid var(--border-accent); border-radius: 16px; all three themes supported; optional hover lift animation
Unified status indicator replacing all manual badge implementations
Semantic colors (emerald/amber/rose/slate); optional pulsing dot for live states; supports: ACTIVE, PENDING, OVERDUE, CLOSED, VALID, INVALID, LIVE, OFFLINE
Bloomberg-style data table base component replacing QuantumRegister for smaller datasets
tabular-nums on all number columns; sticky header; row hover; column sort with animated arrow; virtualized for 1000+ rows; empty/loading states built in
Horizontal scrolling live data ticker for Bloomberg Pro mode header
Continuous CSS animation; configurable items; color-coded values; pause on hover
Animated mesh gradient background for app root and section backgrounds
Subtle radial gradient overlays; changes accent tint based on active businessUnit; CSS animation for subtle movement; respects reduced motion preference
Number that animates from previous value to new value on change
Uses Framer Motion useSpring or vanilla requestAnimationFrame; formats numbers (currency, KG, percentage); color flash on change (counter-tick keyframe)
SVG circular progress ring for tank levels, target completion, shift progress
SVG stroke-dashoffset animation; center label; color changes at thresholds (low=rose, medium=amber, high=emerald)
Spotlight-style search palette (Ctrl+K) for instant navigation to any report or page
Full-width modal overlay; instant fuzzy search across all 232 reports + all navigation items; keyboard navigation; glass morphism panel
Sticky contextual bar showing current shift status, elapsed time, and quick actions
Only visible during active shift; shows elapsed time counter (live), current operator, module indicator; one-click close shift action
HTML element resets, :root variables (light), .dark variables, .bloomberg variables, custom scrollbar, selection color, focus-visible styles
glass, glass-strong, glass-panel, glow-*, gradient-*, mesh-gradient, shimmer, data-positive, data-negative, data-neutral, live-dot, counter-up
card, card-hover, btn (all variants), input (all states), badge (all variants), chip, divider, skeleton, tooltip base styles
shimmer, pulse-dot, pulse-ring, float, draw, shake, gradient-shift, counter-tick — all with reduced-motion @media wrappers
Existing print styles enhanced with proper color-adjust for badges, remove all glow effects, ensure contrast for printed financial reports
--border-accent: rgba(59,130,246,0.25) — dark / rgba(37,99,235,0.15) — light / rgba(245,158,11,0.3) — bloomberg
--glow-blue: rgba(59,130,246,0.35) — used in box-shadow for active states
--glow-emerald: rgba(16,185,129,0.3) — used for positive/success glow states
--glow-rose: rgba(244,63,94,0.3) — used for alert/critical glow states
--mesh-primary: rgba(37,99,235,0.08) — base mesh gradient opacity (Fuel mode)
--mesh-cng: rgba(16,185,129,0.08) — mesh tint in CNG module
--mesh-lube: rgba(245,158,11,0.08) — mesh tint in Lube module
--font-data: "JetBrains Mono", "Consolas", monospace — for all numerical data values
{ bg: "#000000", card: "#0D0D0D", text: "#E5E5E5", accent: "#F5A623", live: "#00D4FF", positive: "#00FF41", negative: "#FF4444" }
{ blue: "#3B82F6", cyan: "#0EA5E9", emerald: "#10B981", rose: "#F43F5E", amber: "#F59E0B", violet: "#7C3AED" }
Minimum color contrast ratio 4.5:1 for all text
All custom CSS variables verified with contrast checker; data-positive/negative meet AA in all three themes
All animations disabled when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) wrapper on all @keyframe animations; Framer Motion useReducedMotion() hook
All interactive elements reachable and operable via keyboard
Custom focus-visible styles (ring-2 in accent color); logical Tab order; Skip to main content link
All icons and animated elements have aria labels
All Lucide icons: aria-label or aria-hidden={true} with adjacent text; loading states: aria-live="polite"
React FocusTrap on all Modal, Sheet, Drawer components
Information not conveyed by color alone
Status badges: color + icon + text label always (never color only)
Minimum 44×44px touch target on all interactive elements
All buttons/icons padded to 44px minimum — especially mobile nav items
Too many blur layers = GPU overload
Use SVG for all icons and illustrations
Required to pass before any PR merge

All phases must be executed in order. Phase N cannot begin until Phase N-1 passes its verification checkpoint. Each phase has explicit deliverables and must not be marked complete until all deliverables are verified by both build check AND visual inspection.
Install all dependencies and establish the design token foundation before touching any component.
Install Framer Motion 11.x: npm install framer-motion
Install JetBrains Mono font: add Google Fonts import or self-host
Extend tailwind.config.js with all tokens from Section 15
Replace entire index.css with the new @layer architecture from Section 14
Verify: npx tsc --noEmit → 0 errors. vite build → success. App still renders correctly.
CHECKPOINT: Open app in browser — themes apply correctly. No visual regressions from existing state.
Implement the full three-mode theme system and all CSS variable tokens.
Add .bloomberg class to index.css with all Bloomberg-mode overrides
Implement theme toggle in GlobalHeader (temporary simple toggle button)
Verify all three modes apply correctly: toggle light → dark → bloomberg → back to light
Verify all CSS custom utility classes render correctly in all three modes
CHECKPOINT: Screenshot sidebar, header, and a data table in all three themes — all must be visually distinct and correct.
The sidebar is the highest-impact visual component. It sets the tone for the entire platform.
Implement Framer Motion <motion.aside> with layout animation for expand/collapse
Add all hover and active state animations per Section 5 specification
Implement business unit switcher with animated pill indicator (layoutId)
Implement mobile drawer with backdrop overlay
Implement user profile card with avatar ring for active shift
Verify expand/collapse at 60fps — no jank. Verify all three themes. Verify mobile drawer.
CHECKPOINT: Video capture of sidebar animation — smooth spring physics, no jitter.
Header scroll-aware shadow, notification badge, theme toggle, and Bloomberg ticker.
Implement sticky glassy header with scroll-aware shadow activation
Implement notification bell with count badge and shake animation
Implement animated theme toggle button (sun/moon/terminal icon with rotation)
Implement Bloomberg Pro mode ticker strip (conditionally shown)
Implement user profile dropdown with slideDown animation
CHECKPOINT: Scroll page — shadow appears. Click bell — badge animates. Toggle theme — icon rotates. Switch to Bloomberg — ticker appears.
AnimatePresence wrapping all routes for smooth cross-page transitions.
Wrap <Outlet /> in <AnimatePresence mode="wait"> with motion.div per route
Implement navigation progress bar (scaleX animation at top of page)
Add mesh gradient background to app container
CHECKPOINT: Navigate between multiple pages — smooth fadeUp transition on every navigation. Progress bar visible. No layout shift.
Premium iOS-style frosted glass mobile navigation with spring animations.
Implement frosted glass bottom bar with safe-area-inset padding
Implement Framer Motion layoutId sliding active pill
Implement whileTap pop animation on all items
Test on actual iOS and Android devices (or browser device emulator)
CHECKPOINT: Tap each nav item — pill slides smoothly. Icons pop on tap. No layout shift. Looks native-quality.
Bloomberg-style data table with semantic color coding, animated sort, skeleton loading.
Add row hover effects and alternating stripe pattern
Implement semantic color classes: data-positive, data-negative, data-neutral
Implement skeleton shimmer loading state (8 rows)
Upgrade DigitalSeal to pulsing emerald glow for VALID state
CHECKPOINT: Open any report — rows hover correctly. Sort column — arrow animates. Loading state shows shimmer. Digital seal glows emerald.
Build all 10 new components from Section 13.
KPICard with animated counter and sparkline
StatusBadge with semantic colors and pulse dot for live states
AnimatedCounter for all financial value displays
CommandPalette (Ctrl+K) with fuzzy search across all 232 reports
Replace all existing manual card/badge implementations with new components
CHECKPOINT: Open dashboard — KPI cards animate counters on load. Ctrl+K opens command palette. Status badges all use unified component.
End-to-end visual verification across all themes, devices, and interaction states.
Visual regression test: all pages in Light mode — screenshot and compare against design spec
Visual regression test: all pages in Dark mode
Visual regression test: all pages in Bloomberg Pro mode
Mobile test: all pages at 375px viewport width
Animation performance test: Chrome DevTools — confirm 60fps on all transitions
Accessibility test: keyboard navigate entire app — no focus traps outside modals
Reduced motion test: enable prefers-reduced-motion — confirm all animations disabled
Build test: npx tsc --noEmit → 0 errors. vite build → 0 warnings. Bundle < 400KB gzipped.
CHECKPOINT: All of the above pass. No known visual bugs. All three themes render perfectly.
Framer Motion tree-shaken; no unexpected large deps
Every item below must be verified by visual inspection in a real browser before the phase is marked complete:
Smooth spring animation, no jitter, correct width
Only icons visible, labels hidden, hover tooltips show
Shadow appears after 10px scroll, disappears at top
Icon rotates 360° on switch, theme applies in <300ms
Continuous horizontal scroll, correct color coding
fadeUp on every route change, progress bar visible
tap animation, pill slides, safe area respected
Pulsing emerald glow, ring expansion animation
Numbers count up from 0 on page mount
Opens glass panel, search works, keyboard navigable
Blur visible, border visible, not too transparent
All animations replaced with instant state change
No horizontal overflow, all controls accessible
All numbers in JetBrains Mono, all data crisp
Framer Motion bundle size increase exceeds 400KB target
Use framer-motion/dom for tree-shaking; lazy-import complex animation components
backdrop-filter not supported on all browsers (Safari iOS)
Test on real iOS devices; provide graceful fallback (solid bg without blur)
60fps animation target not met on low-end Android devices
Use transform/opacity only; add will-change: transform to animated elements; provide reduced-motion fallback
JetBrains Mono font loading causes FOUT
Self-host font files in public/fonts; use font-display: swap; preload in <head>
Bloomberg mode contrast fails WCAG in some color combinations
Test all Bloomberg color combos with contrast checker before Phase 1 complete
AnimatePresence page transitions cause scroll position reset
Use window.scrollTo(0,0) on route change; or preserve scroll per route with ScrollRestoration
CSS variable overrides not applying correctly in .bloomberg class
Add explicit !important to bloomberg-specific overrides; test before Phase 1 closes
Sidebar animation breaks at specific viewport widths
Test at 768px, 1024px, 1440px, 1920px; add unit test for width threshold breakpoints
This document is the final, binding specification for Motorway Oil Enterprise v4.0 UI/UX Architecture Overhaul.
All decisions are made. All components are specified. All tokens are defined. All phases are sequenced.
Execute Phase 0 first. Do not skip. Do not reorder. Do not partially implement.
The result will be a world-class, Bloomberg-quality petroleum enterprise platform — contest-winning by design.
